---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: true
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]

---

```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{Statistical Methods for Forensic Neuropsychology\\ \vspace{1em} \emph{Introduction}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{../Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{../Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{\today}
\maketitle
```


## First slide

-   Presentation of the instructor and the course.
-   Organizational aspects of the course.
-   Learning objectives and "spirit" of the course.

## Second slide (two columns)


```{=latex}
\begin{columns}
\column{0.8\textwidth}
\small
\textbf{The important aspect to remember is that in neuropsychological tests, most often implicitly, the Stevens definition of measurement is assumed and it is possible to assume a measurement on an interval scale.}

\column{0.2\textwidth}
\begin{figure}
\includegraphics[scale=0.05]{../Figures/triangle.png}
\end{figure}
\end{columns}
```




## Slide 3 figure and box

```{=latex}

\begin{tikzpicture}
    % Include your image
    \node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0] (image) at (0,0)
        {\includegraphics[width=0.8\textwidth]{../Figures/Test_Methodology_transp.png}};

    % Set coordinate system to match the image
    \begin{scope}[x={(image.south east)}, y={(image.north west)}]

        % Draw a red rectangle (thin border)
        %\draw[red, thick, rounded corners] (0.25,0.3) rectangle (0.45,0.5);
        % Optional label
        %\node[red, font=\bfseries, above] at (0.35,0.5) {Region 1};

        %\pause
        % a red rectangle (thicker border)
        \draw[red, ultra thick] (0.605,0.65) rectangle (0.905,0.85);

        % text (with fine control)
        \node[anchor=west, xshift=-8mm, yshift=20mm] at (image.east)
        {\parbox{4cm}{\parbox{3cm}{\scriptsize \textbf{Threats to validity}\\ (the test does not measure what it intends to measure)}}};



    \end{scope}
\end{tikzpicture}

```
